mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 0.3% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite consecutive PAYEMS declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth (RSAFS adjusted for inflation via CPIAUCSL) above 0.5% month-over-month signals robust consumer demand and earnings growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 687,641 million and CPIAUCSL at 312.345 as of 2024-03-01
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of instances when real RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month, or Rank IC between real sales growth and 20-day returns < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate rises simultaneously, signaling stagflationary pressures that reduce equity valuations through margin compression and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.2% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.285 and UNRATE at 3.9% as of 2024-03-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when both INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises simultaneously, or the joint interaction term has Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,400 thousand units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2% annualized, signaling demand-side weakness that reduces forward equity returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,400 thousand units AND GDPC1 annualized growth < 2%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,552 thousand and GDPC1 at 23,033.78 billion as of 2024-03-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when both HOUST < 1,400 and GDPC1 growth < 2%, or the interaction coefficient is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates by more than 0.3 percentage points month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, signaling that monetary policy is restrictive relative to inflation dynamics, which supports equity valuations through real rate compression over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change declines > 0.3pp AND FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 312.345 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2024-03-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances when inflation decelerates and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC between real rate proxy and 20-day returns < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate remains below 4.0% for 3+ consecutive months, signaling a tight labor market that supports wage growth, consumer spending, and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: full employment regime with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of instances when UNRATE < 4.0% for 3+ months, or correlation between low unemployment and 60-day SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) grows month-over-month by more than 0.4% while remaining above the 2017=100 baseline, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity that supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.4% AND INDPRO level > 100
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with strong industrial activity
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when INDPRO grows > 0.4% month-over-month, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 20-day returns < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
